items.title: U.S. CISA adds Oracle WebLogic flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
items.description: U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) adds Oracle WebLogic flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) added Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS flaw, tracked as CVE-2024-21182 (CVSS score of 7.5), to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog. The CVE-2024-21182 flaw is an easily exploitable vulnerability affecting Oracle WebLogic […]

items.title: Google Patches Actively Exploited Android Flaw Affecting Millions of Devices
items.description: Google fixed 124 Android flaws, including CVE-2025-48595, an actively exploited privilege escalation bug linked to targeted attacks. Google has released its June 2026 Android security updates, fixing 124 vulnerabilities across the mobile operating system. items.title: Ivanti ITSM Vulnerability Lets Attackers Gain Admin Privilege
items.description:
Ivanti has disclosed a high-severity vulnerability in its Ivanti Neurons for ITSM platform that could allow attackers with valid credentials to escalate privileges and gain full administrative access.  The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-9614, affects both cloud and on-premises deployments and has been assigned a CVSS score of 8.8, indicating a significant security risk in enterprise […]

items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — Attackers Actively Exploiting Critical Vulnerability in Everest Forms Pro Plugin
id: 34c3988e-5dcb-532f-a51b-1f07199d7db2
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to Attackers Actively Exploiting Critical Vulnerability in Everest Forms Pro Plugin (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: LOW | Class: KEV_EXPLOIT
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/Attackers Actively Exploiting Critical Vulnerability in Everest Forms Pro Plugin
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/06/03
modified: 2026/06/03
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: low
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — Attackers Actively Exploiting Critical Vulnerability in Everest Forms Pro Plugin — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-06-03T17:06:17Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "Attackers Actively Exploiting Critical Vulnerability in Everest Forms Pro Plugin", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX Attackers Actively Exploiting Critical Vulnerability in Everest Forms Pro Plugin Exploitation Attempt - KEV_EXPLOIT"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:3447683; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-06-03, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)

items.title: Critical Apache ActiveMQ Vulnerability Allows Malicious Security Header Injections
items.description:
A critical vulnerability in Apache ActiveMQ has been disclosed, allowing attackers to inject malicious HTTP security headers through improperly handled message properties, potentially leading to cross-site scripting and response manipulation attacks in affected deployments. Tracked as CVE-2026-42253, the issue impacts both Apache ActiveMQ and Apache ActiveMQ Web components. The flaw originates from the MessageServlet within […]

items.title: praisonai-platform: Any workspace member can add arbitrary user as owner via POST /workspaces/{id}/members
items.description:
## Summary

**Type:** Privilege escalation / cross-tenant member injection. The `POST /workspaces/{workspace_id}/members` endpoint is gated only by `require_workspace_member(workspace_id)` (default `min_role="member"`) and forwards the request body's `user_id` and `role` straight into `MemberService.add(workspace_id, user_id, role)`, which has no caller-permission check. A user with the lowest workspace privilege can add any user (including a new attacker-controlled second account, or an existing account they want to grief) as owner of the workspace.
**File:** `src/praisonai-platform/praisonai_platform/api/routes/workspaces.py`, lines 92-101; `services/member_service.py`, lines 26-38.
**Root cause:** `MemberService.add` validates only that `role` is in `VALID_ROLES = {"owner", "admin", "member"}` — the value, not the caller's right to assign it. The route's `Depends(require_workspace_member)` resolves to the default `min_role="member"`. So a member-level token plus one POST gives the a
items.apex_ai_summary: [CRITICAL] Vulnerability advisory. Risk score 9.0/10. Full APEX AI analysis available to Pro subscribers.

items.title: Vitest browser mode serves unsanitized otelCarrier query parameter as inline script
items.description:
## Summary

Vitest browser mode served `/__vitest_test__/` with the `otelCarrier` query parameter inserted directly into an inline module script. Because this value was treated as JavaScript source rather than data, an attacker could craft a browser-runner URL that executes arbitrary JavaScript in the Vitest server origin.

https://github.com/vitest-dev/vitest/blob/cba2036a197ec8ed42c35a37db78ef07192202c7/packages/browser/src/node/serverOrchestrator.ts#L48

https://github.com/vitest-dev/vitest/blob/cba2036a197ec8ed42c35a37db78ef07192202c7/packages/browser/src/client/public/esm-client-injector.js#L41

The same generated page embeds `VITEST_API_TOKEN`, which is used to authenticate Vitest WebSocket APIs. Script execution in this origin can therefore recover the token and make authenticated API calls.

## Impact

This issue affects users running Vitest browser mode. A victim must open or navigate to a crafted Vitest browser-runner URL while the Vitest browser server is running.

In the de
